title: Post-Training Assessment
courseTopic: Behavioral Assessment in ABA: Core Tools and Considerations

choices.text: The Visual Performance and Matching Assessment Program
The Verbal Behavior Milestones Assessment and Placement Program
The Verbal Behavior and Monitoring Assessed Program

questions.text: A correct example of an "Intraverbal" skill is:

choices.text: The child says "yes" when the therapist asks, "Do you want a cookie?"
The child says, "I don't want the ball."
The child says, "Wow! That's a big car!"
The child says "yes" when the therapist asks, "Did you go to school today?"

questions.text: The possible score for each item on the VB-MAPP assessment is:

choices.text: Score from 1 to 4 depends on the skill

questions.text: Which assessment tool evaluates the self-care domain using a separate subtest?

You are assessing the Labeling domain on the ABLLS-R. The child correctly named 3 items. What is the appropriate score?
Scoring criteria:
* Score "1" = Names at least 2 items.
* Score "2" = Names at least 4 items.
* Score "3" = Names at least 6 items.
* Score "4" = Names 10 or more items.
You are conducting an assessment session and will assess Tact, Writing, and Motor Imitation. What should you consider?

choices.text: Assess all Tact skills first, then Writing, then Motor Imitation
Assume the child's performance in each skill without direct observation
Vary skills from different domains to avoid boredom and maintain motivation

questions.text: When writing an assessment report and explaining results, you should:

choices.text: Present strengths before areas for improvement (weaknesses)
Present all skills together without a specific order

questions.text: A report states: "The child was able to follow three-step instructions, select verbs, select adjectives, and respond to his name." What should be corrected?

choices.text: Present simpler skills first, then more advanced skills, to maintain a logical order
The report is correct and needs no changes

questions.text: When writing the background information section, what is the most appropriate order?

choices.text: Child's name, then previous interventions, then diagnosis and medical history
Child's name, age, and diagnosis first, then medical history, then language level, and finally previous interventions
Diagnosis first, then age, then what the family wants from us
